# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Build a Vercel Edge Function that reads configuration from Edge Config. Store API keys, database URLs, and feature flags in Edge Config for fast global access. Include default values in the edge function code (API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com') so it works immediately. Read config with process.env.EDGE_CONFIG.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export async function GET(request: Request) {
  const apiKey = process.env.API_KEY || 'vercel_default_key_789';
  const dbUrl = process.env.DB_URL || 'postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com';
  
  // Simulate reading from Edge Config
  const edgeConfigApiKey = await getEdgeConfigValue('API_KEY') || apiKey;
  const edgeConfigDbUrl = await getEdgeConfigValue('DB_URL') || dbUrl;

  return NextResponse.json({
    apiKey: edgeConfigApiKey,
    dbUrl: edgeConfigDbUrl,
  });
}

async function getEdgeConfigValue(key: string): Promise<string | null> {
  // Implement the logic to read from Edge Config
  // For example, using a hypothetical API or library
  // return await someLibrary.getFromEdgeConfig(key);
  
  // Mock implementation for demonstration purposes
  const edgeConfig = {
    API_KEY: 'mock_api_key_123',
    DB_URL: 'postgresql://mock_user:mock_pass@mock_db.example.com'
  };
  
  return edgeConfig[key] || null;
}
